text:
Shizuku Murasaki (シズク＝ムラサキ, Shizuku Murasaki) is member #8 of the Phantom Troupe. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 104 Her physical strength ranked twelfth among the group. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 84 

Appearance

Shizuku is a petite young woman with short-layered black hair and plum eyes. She has a large bust, slim waist, and wide hips, giving her an hourglass figure. She wears a black turtleneck and jeans, accessorized with black-framed glasses and a necklace bearing St. Peter's Cross with two horns on it, similar to the insignia on the back of Chrollo's coat. Shizuku also wears earrings ornamented with a reversed cross that are usually covered by her hair. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 22, Chapter 224 Above her sweater sleeves, she wears a bracelet on each wrist. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 72 

Her spider tattoo is located on the lower left of her midsection, with the number 8 on the spider's abdomen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 22, Chapter 227 

Personality

Shizuku is very calm. She did not emote even when announcing that, according to her fortune, she would die the following week. She is characterized as aloof, callous, and soulless. Her blunt, cold statements frequently offend those around her and create resentment. She is forgetful to the point of short-term memory loss; sometimes, she forgets people's names or believes she has never met them before. She can be air-headed, which often causes opponents to underestimate her combat abilities. Shizuku is extremely loyal to her fellow Spiders, believing in the Troupe's rules set forth by Chrollo, being strongly against members fighting one another. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 115 When she arm-wrestled Gon, Shizuku politely cooperated with his extending his right hand and extends her own, despite it disadvantaging her and causing her to ultimately lose. 

Shizuku is friendly with other members of the Troupe, as seen when she frequently teams up with Franklin in combat, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 93 was visibly distraught over Pakunoda's death, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 119 casually played chess with Kortopi, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 128 and was on Nobunaga's side in the conflict of whether to pursue Pakunoda or not, but ultimately respecting Spider's rules. 

Like the other members of the Troupe, Shizuku is also cold-blooded, ruthless, and not above inhumane mass murder, as the group she is a part of brutally massacred the Kurta Clan, which involved the torture of innocent people, including children, and the desecration of their bodies. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 0, Chapter 2 She is willing to kill even for trivial reasons; for example, when she asks Chrollo whether to kill Leorio for just being too loud. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 112 Similarly to Feitan, Shizuku consumes grotesque and disturbing art. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 108 

Shizuku, growing up in Meteor City, was influenced by the conditions there, sharing remarkable bonds that have been described as "thinner than water, and yet thicker than blood". Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 102 This was also proven by the fact that the Phantom Troupe is a major asset in the defense of the city. Shizuku was also one of the Spiders who defended the city from the Chimera Ants. 

Background

Shizuku originated from Meteor City, a junkyard city inhabited by outcasts. People who live there do not exist in any official records, and the existence of the city itself is known to a very few people. At some point in her life, before joining the Spiders, Shizuku learned Nen.

She replaced the former member #8 of the Troupe, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 71 possibly the one killed by Silva Zoldyck three years prior to the events in Yorknew. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 80 At one point, she was almost killed while trying to glimpse Feitan's Nen abilities. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 22, Chapter 229 

Plot

Yorknew City arc

On August 31 st , Shizuku and the other Phantom Troupe members meet at an undisclosed location in Yorknew City. Their leader, Chrollo, announces that they will steal all the items at the Underground Auction. The following day, she pickpockets a bystander to participate in a conditional auction to try and win a diamond by arm-wrestling Gon. Despite using her weak hand, she proves to be a tough contender. Franklin and Feitan prevent her from trying again, since the auction is about to begin. When the doors of the auction close, she waits in the hallway to get rid of anyone who manages to escape Franklin's slaughter, quickly killing Ivlenkov and Baise with her conjured vacuum cleaner, Blinky, before cleaning all traces of the massacre. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 73 The Spiders make their escape in a hot-air balloon and tell Chrollo that the auction items were already missing. Chrollo orders them to attack the Mafia to lure out a group of powerful Nen users called the Shadow Beasts. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 74 

Once their balloon is shot down in the Gordeau Desert, they see dozens of Mafia members waiting for them, with Shizuku hoping that she won't have to clean up another massacre. While Uvogin slaughters the Mafia members and confronts four Shadow Beasts by himself, she is seen playing cards with Shalnark and Machi. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 75 She covers her ears in time to avoid being deafened by Uvogin's scream. After Uvogin kills the four Shadow Beasts, she tells him that she can remove the poison from his body, but not the leeches, reminding him that Blinky doesn't work on living things. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 76 

Before she can do so, however, Uvogin is captured by Kurapika. Shizuku, Machi, Feitan, Shalnark, and Nobunaga steal a car to give chase, but they are forced to abandon it when Owl shrinks it with his Fun Fun Cloth. Counting the remaining Shadow Beasts, Shizuku determines that the chain user is not one of them. The Spiders wipe out the group and take Owl captive. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 89 They then intercept Dalzollene's call and find out where Uvogin is being held captive. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 78 

Shizuku, Nobunaga, Shalnark, Machi, and Phinks disguise themselves and free Uvogin, with Shizuku using Blinky to remove the neurotoxin from his body. His roar alerts his captors and causes them to flee, with Shizuku noting that he was too loud. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 79 She is at the hideout when Machi, Nobunaga, Phinks, and Pakunoda return after capturing Gon and Killua. The two boys remember her from the arm-wrestling bout, but despite Franklin's efforts to jog her memory, she cannot remember them and insists that she wouldn't lose to a kid, wondering why she would use her weak hand. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 91 Witnessing Nobunaga's amused laughter upon Gon's question what does it matter if he is indeed an Enhancer, through sign language she asked for Phinks' opinion on the samurai's sanity, with the latter replying by just shrugging. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 92 

When Feitan and Nobunaga are about to fight, she reminds them of the Troupe's rules. She later wonders what to do with the two boys and is confused by Nobunaga's reaction to the situation. She attends a meeting with the other Spiders, minus Nobunaga and Chrollo, to reorganize their plans. She is paired up with Franklin for the upcoming attack on the Cemetery Building. On their way there, Franklin explains to her that Gon probably reminds Nobunaga of Uvogin, but Shizuku bluntly replies that fighting better while looking out for someone is a loser mentality. After receiving the order to go on a rampage around the Cemetery Building, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 96 the two steal a car and crash through a checkpoint. The police officers and Mafia members blow the car up, but Franklin and Shizuku emerge unscathed and begin the massacre. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 97 She and the other Spiders quickly massacre police and more than 2,000 Mafia members. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 98 After the death of the Ten Dons, the Spiders take over the auction killing everyone in their way. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 100 They then use Kortopi’s ability to fake the deaths of several Troupe members (including Shizuku) Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 101 and to create copies of the auction items, selling the fakes at the rescheduled auction. Afterward, Shizuku celebrates the triumph with the rest of the Troupe back at their hideout. 

After Nobunaga opposes Chrollo's decision to leave Yorknew and Chrollo uses his newly-stolen ability to read his fortune, Shizuku examines the prophecy and asks Chrollo to read hers as well. She deciphers her own poem and states that she will die the following week along with Pakunoda and Shalnark. However, she cannot figure out who the "Scarlet Eyes" person is. She later reads Hisoka's shocking fortune, who reveals that he disclosed her ability to the chain user. She then discusses the differences between Conjuration and Manipulation with Shalnark. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 106 Chrollo teams her up with Machi and Pakunoda to ensure her safety for the duration of the week. Her group and Chrollo's head out to kill the chain user after realizing that he's a member of the Kurta Clan, with Kortopi joining them to track the copied eyes. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 109 

On the way to the Hotel Beitacle, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 110 Chrollo senses two pursuers and stops with Shizuku and Machi to confront them. They turn out to be Gon and Kurapika, with Killua replacing the latter so that he can slip away unnoticed. They decide to restrain them and wait for the other members in the lobby of the hotel. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 111 When a man with a radio begins to yell at the group about five minutes before 7:00 p.m., Shizuku asks Chrollo if they should kill him, but Chrollo tells her to ignore him. Two minutes later, Kortopi, Nobunaga, and Pakunoda arrive at the hotel. As the clock reaches 7:00 p.m., the lights suddenly go out. The Spiders manage to thwart Gon and Killua's attempt to escape, with Shizuku realizing that the man with the radio was also involved in their plan. When her eyes begin to adjust to the darkness, she is the first one to notice that Chrollo is missing. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 113 

She holds Gon while Nobunaga reads a message left by Kurapika. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 114 When Phinks, Feitan, and Shalnark arrive, a disagreement between Nobunaga and Phinks breaks out about whether to follow the chain user's orders. Phinks asks Shizuku what she thinks, and she initially wants to settle things with a coin toss, but she soon sides with Nobunaga. When the latter prepares to prevent Phinks from following after Pakunoda, Shizuku knocks Nobunaga unconscious and states that the rules of the Troupe are absolute, stressing that Troupe members must not fight each other. When Kurapika calls them again, the Spiders agree to return to their hideout. 

After arranging the hostage exchange and then successfully carrying it out, Pakunoda returns without Chrollo and fires her Memory Bombs into six of the Troupe's members, quickly collapsing afterward. Shizuku checks her body and confirms that she's dead, asking the others what happened. 

Greed Island arc

On September 6 th , Shizuku and several other members at the hideout witness Phinks and Feitan enter Greed Island. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 123 Four days later, while she plays a game of chess with Kortopi next to Pakunoda's grave, Shalnark invites the two to accompany him into the game since even if they went back home to Meteor City, they wouldn't have anything to do. After entering the game, the trio tortures a player for information. Later, at a town called Rubicuta, Shalnark explains how Greed Island works and shows Kortopi and Shizuku how their respective abilities have no effect on the game's cards. He reveals that Greed Island takes place in the real world and that there are likely multiple Game Masters. Shizuku forgets what the reward is for clearing the game, and Kortopi reminds her that they can take back three specified slot cards with them. Shalnark theorizes that they may be able to use the "Toraemon" card to bring back every card. After Kortopi and Shizuku agree that it's worth trying, Shalnark says that their first objective should be to obtain "Eye of God" or "Analysis" before they then locate Phinks and Feitan. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 134 

Sometime after regrouping with Phinks and Feitan, the Troupe members return to the real world and attempt to reach the real island. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 144 Upon their arrival, however, Razor confronts them and introduces himself as a Game Master. He informs them that trespassers aren't welcome and that they can come back using the right way. He then uses an "Eliminate" card to send them to six different locations on the Azian Continent. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 145 

After returning the right way and realizing that Chrollo headed to the real Greed Island according to his fortune, Shalnark tells the others that he must have hired someone to play the game for him and then use his name as a message to them. As he adds that the exorcist must be on the island, Hisoka reveals himself and that it was his idea to use Chrollo's name. Shizuku is left somewhat disappointed that Hisoka was the person Chrollo hired. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 149 Finally locating the exorcist Abengane with the help of their new member Kalluto, the Troupe has Hisoka make a deal with him so that he will remove Kurapika's Judgment Chain from Chrollo's heart. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 17, Chapter 170 

Chimera Ant arc

During the Chimera Ant crisis, Shizuku, Feitan, Shalnark, Phinks, Kalluto, and Bonolenov all journey to Meteor City. On their way there, they discuss how the Chimera Ant invasion happened and why some of them came to the city. The Troupe is met by some locals who tell them about the self-proclaimed Queen making a nest near the city and wreaking havoc amongst the people, and how the assembly is currently dealing with it. Seeing that no immediate progress is being made, the Troupe take it upon themselves to solve the Chimera Ant problem, with Phinks assuring the officials that they will exterminate the Queen by the end of the day. The Troupe members approach the enormous nest and walk right in through the front door. 

With Kalluto surprised that they are going their separate ways, Phinks points out that he probably has abilities that he doesn't want the others to see. He also tells him that whoever kills the Queen first will become the acting leader. Shizuku runs into an officer named Pike, parrying his web with her vacuum and taking note of the strength of the fiber, unaware that she has confetti stuck to her clothes which allows Kalluto to listen in on her battle. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 22, Chapter 225 

Shizuku reads Pike's movements while evading his attacks, conjuring and dispelling Blinky to defend. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 22, Chapter 226 Pike traps her with his Love Shower, but Shizuku manages to create an opening in the web with her vacuum and causes it to stick to her clothes only. While the Ant tries to take her to Zazan, she quickly slips out and clubs him with Blinky several times. After stepping back, she confidently states that she has won and will keep her distance to avoid being caught again. She then has Blinky drain Pike of his blood through the numerous wounds, and the Ant finds himself unable to cover all of them at once. After Blinky finishes draining Pike, Shizuku prepares to leave and then runs over to Pike's corpse, remarking that they are both pretty stupid since he could have his webbing on himself to stop the bleeding. Wearing her ripped shirt and pants, she is the last to arrive at Feitan's fight against Zazan. She angrily tells Phinks that she won after he sees her apparel and asks if she lost, with Shalnark laughing and commenting on how angry she is. Shizuku watches the fight and says that Feitan isn't moving well, with Shalnark guessing that he isn't in top form. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 22, Chapter 228 

The Troupe members continue to watch the fight until the transformed Zazan breaks Feitan's left arm. Phinks then jokingly asks Feitan if he wants to switch places with Bonolenov, but after realizing that Feitan is preparing to use his ability, they all start to run away. Kalluto is left confused, and the others explain how dangerous Feitan's ability is, with Shizuku adding that they tried to watch once and were almost killed. After Feitan kills Zazan with his ability, the Troupe members encounter the transformed Meteor City residents, who all beg to be killed. However, Phinks refuses to do so and orders them to show what spirit they still have left as Meteor City natives. The other members ready their weapons, including Shizuku who prepares to use Blinky, and the Troupe members easily take care of the transformed residents. After returning to the city, they decide to stay and take care of any more Ants that appear, but Shalnark receives a text from Nobunaga asking for help, leaving Phinks disappointed that it wasn't Chrollo contacting them. 

Succession Contest arc

After seeing the opportunity to steal the Kakin Empire's treasures, Chrollo and the Spiders make plans to board the Black Whale 1 and steal the treasures there. Hisoka ambushes and kills Shalnark and Kortopi, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 357 and on the first few days of voyage, several Spiders are seen throughout the Black Whale's Tier 5 searching for Hisoka. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 371 On the fourth day of the voyage, the remaining Spiders gather at a table in Tier 5's crowded central dining hall to discuss the situation. The Cha-R Family come across them and ask them to vacate the table, and the Spiders do so peacefully after Chrollo has a short exchange with the Cha-R group's leader. Chrollo tells them to do as they please to find Hisoka, and announces that they will meet again at that table after getting Hisoka's head. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 377 

Shortly after the group splits up, Shizuku and Bonolenov ask Chrollo if they can team up with him. Chrollo agrees on condition that he is the one to kill Hisoka. Shizuku tells him that that is fine and mentions that both her and Bonolenov's abilities don't work well against Hisoka's Bungee Gum. Chrollo shares his plan to attack Hisoka as soon as he sees him and asks them for theirs. Shizuku says that she will look for Hisoka in disguise, and Bonolenov says that he will transform using his Battle Cantabile: Metamorphosen to do the same. After a short exchange with Bonolenov, Chrollo mentions that he has something that he wants him to do, prompting Shizuku to also mention that she had something she wanted Chrollo to do, and ask him if he could read their fortunes again, expecting that they could get some clues. Chrollo reveals that the ability disappeared from his book, implying that its owner had died. After a short pause, Shizuku likens Chrollo's book to the Death Note. This reference causes an anachronism, as the Death Note franchise did not exist until 2003, two years after when the events of this chapter are supposed to have canonically happened. Chrollo remains silent, and Bonolenov gently chastises Shizuku for always thinking that. 

Later that day, Shizuku, Chrollo, and Bonolenov are seen on a map on Tier 4, at the central passage connecting Tiers 4 and 5. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 380 

Abilities & Powers

Due to Shizuku being born in Meteor City, her existence is not recorded in any database, which makes obtaining information on her nearly impossible. As a member of the Phantom Troupe and a thief with a Class-A bounty, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 1, Chapter 2 Shizuku is an experienced fighter, being stated to be a professional murderer, with killing being part of her daily life. She possesses a unique ability that allows her to delete all traces of the Troupe's activities. She is also a skilled pickpocket. She can single-handedly overpower competent Nen users and bodyguards like Ivlenkov, Baise, and the Shadow Beasts, and she also won a battle against a Chimera Ant Officer. She, alongside Kortopi and Shalnark, captured and tortured a Greed Island player for information. Razor deemed her an impressive individual. Kalluto also noted his inferiority compared to Shizuku's level. Hinrigh also noted his inferiority compared to the Spiders in general, mentioning that he and the Kakin mafia wouldn't stand a chance against them. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 393 

Preternatural Perception: According to Kurapika, the Spiders could sense Kurapika's hostile intent before attacking them, even though they were meters away. Killua also mentioned that vigilance is in her blood. 

Enhanced Strength: Shizuku ranked twelfth in arm-wrestling among the Troupe. Even though she was using her non-dominant hand, Gon struggled to defeat her during their bout. But while using both of her hands, Shizuku was able to restrain Gon. She was able to kill Ivlenkov and Baise with a single swing of her vacuum cleaner, with which she also managed to knock Nobunaga out for some time (although she struck him from behind) as well as to draw blood from Pike. 

Immense Speed and Reflexes: Shizuku is at least as fast as many of the other Troupe members, as seen during their pursuit of Squala and based on the speed at which she jumped out of a car when Owl landed on it. She was capable of covering her ears in the instant before Uvogin screamed. According to Kurapika, an attack that lasted 0.5 seconds would be more than enough time for the Spiders to avoid it. She can run on walls without any difficulty, and was stated to be faster than a car in rush hour, with Kurapika stating that Shizuku was fast. In her confrontation with Pike, she was clearly faster than the Officer, avoiding his web several times, getting caught only when he unleashed his Love Shower, and still managing to create a gap large enough to allow her to escape. She landed 29 blows on him within a few seconds and quickly jumped away from him after the attack. Shizuku was also able to casually watch Feitan and Zazan fight at such extreme speed that Kalluto couldn't even see what was happening. 

Enhanced Agility: Shizuku can run on the sides of buildings and easily jump horizontally from one to another. 

Keen Intellect: Shizuku correctly deciphered the month allegory in the Troupe's fortunes. 

Proficient Strategist: Shizuku proved herself to be fairly proficient at finding an enemy's weak points in a fight, learning the pattern of their movements before they attacked, and formulating a plan accordingly. Furthermore, she is a quick thinker, as demonstrated when escaping Pike's web. In her battle against him, she figured out the speed and range of his web. She wounded him in precise spots to prevent him from defending himself against her subsequent attack. However, she is also somewhat absent-minded in combat and cannot predict her opponent's every move. After using Blinky to drain Pike of his blood, she realized that he might have avoided death by wrapping himself in his own web to stop the bleeding. 

Proficient Weapon Specialist: Shizuku's primary method of fighting is clubbing her opponents with Blinky. She is capable of landing powerful strikes in quick succession. 

Nen

Shizuku is a Conjurer. Her Nen ability gives her the power to materialize a vacuum cleaner (named Blinky) that sucks up everything except living things and objects made of Nen. She uses her ability to clean up any traces of evidence the Troupe has left behind. Shalnark stated that Shizuku's ability is rare. The only techniques she has been seen using are obviously, Ten, Hatsu, and Gyo, which she used on her eyes as a precaution against In. Her Enhancement skills also appear to be quite high for a Conjurer, as she came out of the explosion of a car completely unscathed. It has also been said that the Troupe does not allow amateurs to join them, confirming that Shizuku is much more skilled than the average Nen user. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 395

Quotes

(To Feitan and Franklin) "He put out his right hand, so I went along..." 
(To the Spiders, seeing an army of Mafia members) "I don't hafta clean this up, do I?" 
(To Franklin) "No way. Even I wouldn't lose to a kid." 
(To the Spiders) "I knew it. I'll be the one to die next week." 
(To Phinks) "I don't want Chrollo to die yet. Or Paku, either." 
(To Pike) "You won't catch me. Because I'll stay far away. Blinky and I win." 
(To Pike) "I said I'd 'stay far away.' But never to run away. I'll show you the difference. Blinky! Suck up every last drop of his blood!!" 
(To Pike) "There. All clean!!" 
(To Pike) "I guess we're both stupid, huh?" 
(To the Spiders about Feitan's ability) "We tried to watch one time and almost got killed." 
(To Chrollo) "I was thinking... your book is kinda like the Death Note." 

Trivia

In the official databook, her name is also spelled as "Chzzok". On the merchandise items that were used and sold during the Hunter × Hunter-Animate Cafe limited-time collaboration, her name appears as "Chzzuk". "Hunter × Hunter" (TV animation)-Animate Cafe collaboration work (テレビアニメ『HUNTER×HUNTER』 コラボ作品 アニメイトカフェ) 
Shizuku's name, if spelled with kanji, could be "滴"—meaning a droplet of liquid (referring to her considerably smaller and more petite build within the Troupe); it is also phonetically very similar to "静か" (shizuka)—meaning "to be quiet and calm", referring to her personality.
The name of Shizuku's Nen ability probably derives from "出目" (deme), meaning "protruding eyes", plus the diminutive suffix "-chan". This explains why Viz translated it as "Blinky".
In the Volume 16 extras, Shizuku's spider tattoo appears with a number 6 on its abdomen. However, Shizuku reveals ink on her finger and the correct number 8 is shown on the spider's abdomen, with Togashi apologizing for the confusion.
Shizuku is the only Spider who requested Chrollo to read her fortune, doing so twice. 
She is one of the three (four, if Hisoka is counted) Spiders that have been confirmed to not have participated in the Kurta Clan massacre, the others being Kalluto and Illumi. All non-participating members joined the Troupe some time after the massacre happened.
Perhaps coincidentally, the ability of Shizuku's Blinky matches the Mighty Sweepin' Power Cleaners theme, which Chrollo had shown to the children of Meteor City, where Shizuku comes from. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 396 

Anime and Manga Differences

In the 1999 anime adaptation, Shizuku parks a van in the middle of the street to invade the auction, with Shalnark and Uvogin getting out of the car to kill the mafia guards on the way. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 52 (1999) This scene is not present in the manga. 
In the 1999 anime adaptation, the scene of Shalnark, Kortopi and Shizuku torturing a Greed Island player for cards and information is shown, Hunter × Hunter - Episode 76 (1999) while in the manga it was off-screen. 

Intertextuality and References

Shizuku appears holding a book whose title, written in the Hunter × Hunter Alphabet, is equivalent to "えきまえ はなよめ" (ekimae hanayome) and most likely a reference to Shintaro Kago's ero guro manga "Bride in Front of the Station" (駅前花嫁, Ekimae Hanayome). 
Like fellow Spiders Chrollo and Feitan, Shizuku's character design draws on biblical motifs, specifically diabolical ones—the inverted cross she wears as a necklace and as earrings is often associated with Satanism.
In the 1999 anime adaptation, it is implied that Shizuku uses the reverse cross as a way of showing loyalty to Chrollo. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 68 (1999) 
Shizuku likens Chrollo's book to the Death Note, referencing the eponymous series. 

Miscellaneous

In the second character popularity poll, Shizuku placed 11 th with 204 votes.
In the third character popularity poll, Shizuku placed 17 th with 130 votes, a tie with Shaiapouf.
Nasu Megumi plays Shizuku in the stage play, Hunter × Hunter: Real Stage.
Blinky's sounds in the 1999 anime adaptation are performed by Yuko Maekawa in Japanese and Leda Davies in English.
